content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer NNN. In one operation you can:


Either subtract 333 or subtract 444 from NNN.
Can you make NNN zero by performing any number of operations?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains a positive integer NNN, denoting the integer which you need to make zero.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if you can make NNN zero using any number of operations. Otherwise, print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: You cannot make N=1N=1N=1 zero by performing any number of operations.
Test case 222: Subtract 333 from N=3N=3N=3 to make it zero in one operation.
Test case 333: Subtract 444 from N=7N=7N=7 to make it N=3N=3N=3 in one operation. Then, subtract 333 from N=3N=3N=3 to make it zero in second operation.
